content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6

## Summary

Independently verified the amendment by reading all relevant code paths. The bug is confirmed: (1) successful run sets RunStatus::Completed in the snapshot, (2) reconciliation failure at lines 1496-1522 marks the daemon task Failed and releases the lease, (3) retry_task at task_service.rs:476 only transitions task status to Pending without resetting the run snapshot, (4) next dispatch reads RunStatus::Completed at line 2373-2375 and dispatch_in_worktree rejects it at lines 3007-3010 with TaskStateTransitionInvalid. The comments at lines 1608-1616 and 1755-1762 explicitly promise retry will re-dispatch reconciliation, but the code makes this impossible. Any transient reconciliation error (network failure during br sync, milestone store I/O error) produces a permanent fail→retry→fail loop.

## Votes

- `fr-12-4b0289eb`: Accept
  Confirmed through independent code reading. The dispatch function (line 2958-3012) has exactly four match arms: NotStarted, Failed|Paused, Running, Completed. The Completed arm unconditionally returns TaskStateTransitionInvalid. There is no path from a retried task back into reconciliation code — retry_task only resets task status to Pending, not the run snapshot. The comments at lines 1608-1616 and 1755-1762 promise that retry_task will 're-dispatch reconciliation against the still-Running milestone', but this is factually incorrect: the run snapshot already shows Completed, and dispatch rejects Completed runs before any reconciliation logic can execute. This is not a theoretical concern — any transient failure in br sync, br close, or milestone store updates during reconciliation will permanently wedge the bead/milestone with no automated recovery. The fix (either a dedicated reconciliation-only retry path that bypasses the run status gate, or resetting run status so retry can re-enter dispatch) is clearly needed.
